repo.description: The most widely used Python to C compiler
repo.name: cython

body:
This PR uses `StringSourceDescriptor` instead of empty pyx file in temp dir. This simplifies the logic and also ensures that __pyx_f is constant across builds.

Alternative to #7634

comment_data.body: I think we intentionally left out the file methods at the time to prevent accidental mixups between the two. Not sure how large the risk of that is. If we allow both to be used interchangeably, then that would also remove that risk.
comment_data.diff_hunk:
@@ -278,6 +282,10 @@ def get_relative_path(self):
 
     get_error_description = get_description
 
+    def get_file_object(self, encoding=None, error_handling=None):
+        from io import StringIO
+        return StringIO('\n'.join(self.codelines))

comment_data.body: This exception really makes no sense if we now allow both of the types that it's trying to distinguish.
comment_data.diff_hunk:
@@ -375,7 +375,7 @@ def find_submodule(self, name, as_package=False):
         return scope
 
     def parse(self, source_desc, scope, pxd, full_module_name):
-        if not isinstance(source_desc, FileSourceDescriptor):
+        if not isinstance(source_desc, (FileSourceDescriptor, StringSourceDescriptor)):
             raise RuntimeError("Only file sources for code supported")

comment_data.body:
I hadn't actually realized that the file was completely empty and only existed so there was a file...

The new version seems pretty straightforward to me, especially knowing this.
comment_data.diff_hunk:
@@ -71,23 +68,17 @@ def generate_shared_module(options):
     Errors.reset()
 
     dest_c_file = options.shared_c_file_path
+    pyx_file = os.path.splitext(dest_c_file)[0] + '.pyx'
     module_name = os.path.splitext(os.path.basename(dest_c_file))[0]
 
     context = Main.Context.from_options(options)
     scope = Symtab.ModuleScope('MemoryView', parent_module = None, context = context, is_package=False)
 
-    with tempfile.TemporaryDirectory() as tmpdirname:
-        pyx_file = os.path.join(tmpdirname, f'{module_name}.pyx')
-        c_file = os.path.join(tmpdirname, f'{module_name}.c')
-        with open(pyx_file, 'w'):
-            pass

comment_data.body:
This method isn't needed, unless you want to explicitly remove the ability to pass a `path_description` argument.

I looked through the usages to see what that `path_description` does and it seems that no-one really knew for quite a while. :) The usages pass some kind of relative path into it which the class then converts into a relative path again and stores away for display purposes. This behaviour probably emerged from the various attempts to use relative paths in C source files. Either way, it's worth cleaning up at some point (not in this PR).
comment_data.diff_hunk:
@@ -294,6 +294,18 @@ def __repr__(self):
         return "<StringSourceDescriptor:%s>" % self.name
 
 
+class SharedUtilitySourceDescriptor(FileSourceDescriptor):
+    """
+    A specialized source descriptor for shared utility code only. Not part of public API.
+    """
+
+    def __init__(self, name):
+        super().__init__(name)

commit.message: Shared module: Use StringSourceDescriptor instead of temporary directory.
